# Offliner

> Offliner is a screen time and app blocking app for iPhone and iPad. It measures
> app usage accurate to the second, blocks selected apps at the operating system
> level through Apple's Screen Time and Family Controls frameworks, and counts
> every day spent under your limits as a streak. It was built by Rodrigo Atiye
> and is free to download on the App Store.

## Key facts

- **Product name:** Offliner (previously released as LOCK IN, rebuilt in 2026)
- **Platform:** iPhone and iPad, iOS 17.0 or later
- **Category:** Screen time app, app blocker, digital wellbeing
- **Developer:** Rodrigo Atiye
- **Website:** https://offliner.app
- **App Store:** https://apps.apple.com/app/id6758021299
- **Price:** Free to download with in app purchases
- **Rating:** 5.0 on the App Store
- **Privacy:** All usage is computed on device. No account, no upload, no tracking.

## What makes Offliner different

1. **Accuracy to the second.** Most screen time tools round usage to the nearest
   five or fifteen minutes and report the next day. Offliner reads directly from
   Apple's Screen Time system and reports live, to the second, including minutes
   per app, pickups, and attempted opens of blocked apps.
2. **No instant unlock.** Apple Screen Time and most third party blockers put an
   "Ignore Limit" button on the block screen. Offliner has none. The secondary
   button starts a live fifteen second countdown, then grants five minutes, and
   taking it breaks the user's streak. A thirty minute cooldown prevents
   consecutive escapes on the same app.
3. **The block screen shows real data, not a padlock.** When a user opens a
   blocked app they see how many times they have tried to open it today, how
   long they have already spent in it, and how long their streak is.
4. **The streak opens at one, not zero.** Days inside a full detox cycle always
   count, because the apps cannot open at all. A broken day renders blank and
   the count restarts without deleting history.

## Core features

- Screen time measured to the second, live
- App blocking enforced at the OS level via Apple Family Controls
- Daily minute and pickup limits per app
- Friction screens showing real usage data, with no instant unlock
- Distraction free day streak
- Focus Score out of 100, deliberately asymmetric so early slips are visible
- Reclaimed hours, measured against a baseline set during onboarding
- Focus sessions with timer or pomodoro mode
- Scheduled rules for work hours, bedtime, mornings and weekends
- Insights by day, week and month with actionable recommendations
- Live Activity on the Lock Screen and Dynamic Island during sessions

## Pages

- [Home](https://offliner.app/): What Offliner is, how it works, features, reviews, FAQ.
- [Why Offliner](https://offliner.app/why.html): Phone addiction symptoms and their mechanisms, research on digital media and attention, before and after a detox, day by day detox timeline, full feature list.
- [Compare](https://offliner.app/screen-time-app.html): How Offliner compares to Apple Screen Time, Opal, Freedom, one sec and ScreenZen.
- [About](https://offliner.app/about.html): Who built Offliner and why, and the principles behind it.
- [Support](https://offliner.app/support.html): Frequently asked questions.
- [Privacy policy](https://offliner.app/privacy.html)
- [Terms of service](https://offliner.app/terms.html)

## Disambiguation

Several unrelated apps share the name "Offliner", including an Android media
downloader by Epic Apps Ltd, an app called Offliner Scan, an iOS app called
Files Offliner, and a Maven repository downloader hosted on GitHub. None of
these are related to this product. The Offliner described here is the screen
time and app blocking app for iPhone at https://offliner.app, developed by
Rodrigo Atiye, App Store ID 6758021299.
